evidence.name: Murder Weapon (Kitchen Knife)
evidence.description: A bloody kitchen knife found in the victim's kitchen sink. Blood matches the victim.

evidence.name: Two Wine Glasses on Table
evidence.description: Two wine glasses on the living room table. Indicates the victim had company before the murder.

evidence.name: Julia's Fingerprint on Wine Glass
evidence.description: Fingerprint analysis of the wine glass reveals Julia Ross's fingerprints. Contradicts her statement that she was not at the apartment that night.
evidence.lab_result_text: Fingerprint matched to Julia Ross. Clear ridge patterns confirm identity.
evidence.hint_text: Have the wine glasses analyzed for fingerprints at the lab.

evidence.name: Fingerprint on Desk (Unanalyzed)
evidence.description: A clear fingerprint found on the victim's desk. Needs lab analysis to identify the owner.

evidence.name: Mark's Fingerprint on Desk
evidence.description: Fingerprint analysis of the victim's desk reveals Mark Bennett's prints. Confirms his earlier visit.

evidence.name: Broken Picture Frame
evidence.description: A broken picture frame on the floor. The photo shows Daniel and Julia together. Suggests an argument took place.

evidence.name: Victim's Phone
evidence.description: Daniel's phone found on the table. Contains messages and call history.

evidence.name: Text Message From Julia
evidence.description: A text message from Julia to Daniel at 20:40: "Are you home? We need to talk."
evidence.hint_text: Check the victim's phone for recent messages.

evidence.name: Deleted Messages (Recovered)
evidence.description: Recovered deleted messages between Daniel and Julia showing a heated argument about money. Unlocked via phone search warrant.
evidence.hint_text: A warrant to search Julia's phone may reveal deleted messages.

evidence.name: Call Log Between Mark and Daniel
evidence.description: Phone call log showing multiple calls between Mark and Daniel about financial issues in the days before the murder.

evidence.name: Email From Daniel to Mark
evidence.description: An email from Daniel to Mark. Subject: "We need to fix this before tomorrow." References financial irregularities.

evidence.name: Suspicious Bank Transfer
evidence.description: Records showing money moved from the company account to an unknown destination. Suggests embezzlement.
evidence.hint_text: Check the file cabinet in Daniel's office for financial records.

evidence.name: Accounting Files
evidence.description: Detailed accounting files showing a pattern of embezzlement from the company. Found in the victim's office.

evidence.name: Julia's Financial Records
evidence.description: Julia's personal financial records showing large debts. Provides additional financial motive.

evidence.name: Parking Lot Camera Footage
evidence.description: Security camera footage from the parking lot showing Mark Bennett leaving the building at 20:40.
evidence.hint_text: Check the security camera in the parking lot.

evidence.name: Hallway Camera (Blurry Figure)
evidence.description: Security camera footage showing someone entering the apartment at approximately 20:50. The figure's height roughly matches Julia Ross.

evidence.name: Elevator Logs
evidence.description: Building elevator access logs showing Julia Ross's key card was used at 20:48 on the night of the murder.
evidence.hint_text: Check the building's elevator access records.

evidence.name: Sarah's Testimony
evidence.description: Sarah Klein's initial statement: she heard an argument around 20:45 but claims she did not see anyone in the hallway.

evidence.name: Sarah's Second Testimony
evidence.description: Under pressure, Sarah admits hearing a female voice during the argument. She also remembers hearing quick footsteps in the hallway.

evidence.name: Lucas Work Log
evidence.description: Maintenance work log showing Lucas Weber was working until 20:00, contradicting his claim of finishing at 19:00. However, he was in a different part of the building.

evidence.name: Shoe Print in Hallway (Unanalyzed)
evidence.description: A shoe print found in the hallway near the victim's apartment. Needs lab analysis to determine the shoe type and size.

evidence.name: Shoe Print in Hallway
evidence.description: A shoe print found in the hallway near the victim's apartment. Lab analysis matches Julia Ross's shoes.
evidence.lab_result_text: Shoe print pattern matches a pair of women's shoes, size 38. Consistent with Julia Ross's shoe size.
evidence.hint_text: Examine the hallway floor for footprints.

evidence.name: Julia's Shoes (Search Warrant)
evidence.description: Julia's shoes obtained via search warrant. The soles match the hallway shoe print exactly.
evidence.hint_text: A search warrant for Julia's residence may yield matching shoes.

evidence.name: Wine Bottle
evidence.description: A recently opened bottle of wine on the table. Confirms the victim was sharing drinks with someone.

evidence.name: Knife Block in Kitchen
evidence.description: A knife block on the kitchen counter with one knife missing. The missing knife matches the murder weapon.

evidence.name: Hidden Safe in Office
evidence.description: A hidden safe found in Daniel's office containing documents about the embezzlement scheme. Reveals the full extent of Mark's financial crimes and Daniel's plan to expose him.
evidence.hint_text: Look carefully around Daniel's office — there may be hidden compartments.

evidence.name: Daniel's Personal Journal
evidence.description: Daniel's personal journal found in his office. Recent entries mention confronting both Mark about the embezzlement and Julia about their marriage. The last entry reads: "I have to tell Julia everything tomorrow."
evidence.hint_text: Daniel may have kept personal notes at his office.
